2605.03439.title: Benchmarking Logistic Regression, SVM, Naive Bayes, and IndoBERT Fine-Tuning for Sentiment Analysis on Indonesian Product Reviews
2605.03439.paper_summary: The exponential growth of e-commerce platforms in Indonesia has generated a massive volume of user-generated product reviews. Analyzing the sentiment of these reviews is critical for measuring customer satisfaction and identifying product issues at scale. This paper benchmarks traditional Machine Learning (ML) approaches against a Transformer-based Deep Learning model for a three-class sentiment analysis task (positive, neutral, negative) on the Tokopedia Product Reviews 2025 dataset. We implemented Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) feature extraction coupled with three algorithms: Logistic Regression, Linear Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Multinomial Naive Bayes as robust baselines. Subsequently, we fine-tuned the IndoBERT model (indobenchmark/indobert-base-p1) for contextual sequence classification. To computationally address the severe class imbalance inherent in e-commerce feedback, we applied balanced class weights for the baseline models and engineered a custom weighted cross-entropy loss function within the IndoBERT training loop, following the broader motivation of imbalanced-learning research. Our comprehensive evaluation using Accuracy, Macro F1-score, and Weighted F1-score revealed that the traditional Linear SVC model significantly outperformed the IndoBERT model in our experimental setup, achieving an Accuracy of 97.60% and a Macro F1-score of 0.5510, compared to IndoBERT's 88.70% and 0.5088. Detailed analysis indicates that this performance gap was primarily driven by discrepancies in the data sampling regimes, where baselines utilized the full corpus while the Transformer was constrained to a sampled subset. Finally, we demonstrate the practical viability of our pipeline by deploying the final sentiment classification model as an interactive Gradio web application.
2605.03439.paper_summary_zh: 印尼電子商務平台的指數增長產生了大量用戶生成的產品評論。分析這些評論的情感對於衡量客戶滿意度和識別產品問題至關重要。本文將傳統的機器學習（ML）方法與基於Transformer的深度學習模型進行基準測試，針對Tokopedia產品評論2025數據集進行三類情感分析任務（正面、中立、負面）。我們實施了詞頻-逆文檔頻率（TF-IDF）特徵提取，並結合了三種算法：邏輯回歸、線性支持向量機（SVM）和多項式朴素貝葉斯作為穩健的基準。隨後，我們對IndoBERT模型（indobenchmark/indobert-base-p1）進行了微調，以進行上下文序列分類。為了計算性地解決電子商務反饋中固有的嚴重類別不平衡問題，我們對基準模型應用了平衡類別權重，並在IndoBERT訓練循環中設計了一個自定義加權交叉熵損失函數，這符合不平衡學習研究的更廣泛動機。我們使用準確率、宏觀F1分數和加權F1分數進行的綜合評估顯示，在我們的實驗設置中，傳統的線性SVC模型顯著優於IndoBERT模型，達到了97.60%的準確率和0.5510的宏觀F1分數，而IndoBERT則為88.70%和0.5088。詳細分析表明，這一性能差距主要是由於數據抽樣方案的差異造成的，基準模型使用了完整的語料庫，而Transformer則受到限制於一個抽樣子集。最後，我們通過將最終情感分類模型部署為互動式Gradio網頁應用程序，展示了我們管道的實際可行性。
2605.03439.author: Nabila Zakiyah Zahra et.al.
2605.03439.authors: Nabila Zakiyah Zahra, Salwa Farhanatussaidah, Nasywa Nur Afifah, Luluk Muthoharoh, Ardika Satria, Martin C. T. Manullang
